---
title: "Life Expectancy and Population"
---

## Introduction

This page explores how life expectancy relates to population size in the Gapminder dataset.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

``` 

```{python}
# Scatter plot: Population vs Life expectancy
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["population_millions"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("Population (millions)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs Population")
plt.show()

``` 

## Interpretation

The scatterplot shows that there isn't a strong linear relationship between population size and life expectancy. Unlike GDP per capita, population alone does not appear to strongly predict life expectancy.
